events.summary: Strong engineering representation. Moderate relocation flexibility. Leadership identified 4 standout candidates. Event showed moderate conversion from RSVP to high-potential pipeline.

detail.education: MBA, Washington University in St. Louis – Finance & Strategy
detail.opsExposure: Led cross-functional supply chain optimization at a Fortune 500 manufacturer. Managed $12M logistics budget.
detail.leadershipDepth: Supervised a team of 8 analysts. Drove quarterly planning cycles across 3 regional offices.
detail.leaderNotes: Exceptional strategic thinker. Very strong communicator—stood out in group exercise. Recommended by 2 leaders independently.
detail.resumeSummary: 5 years progressive experience in operations and strategy. Strong analytical background with demonstrated P&L ownership. Published internal white paper on lean methodology adoption.

detail.opsExposure: Process improvement engineer at mid-size manufacturer. Led Kaizen events and implemented 5S across two facilities.
detail.leadershipDepth: Informal team lead for 4-person continuous improvement squad. Presented results to plant leadership monthly.
detail.leaderNotes: Strong technical foundation. Needs more leadership seasoning but shows high ceiling. Good culture fit.
detail.resumeSummary: 3 years in manufacturing engineering. Six Sigma Green Belt certified. Reduced defect rate by 22% on flagship product line.

detail.education: BS Business Administration, Saint Louis University
detail.opsExposure: Rotational analyst in corporate planning. Limited direct operations exposure but strong analytical toolkit.
detail.leadershipDepth: No direct reports. Active in campus leadership—student body VP, case competition captain.
detail.leaderNotes: Promising but early career. Needs more operational reps before POD consideration. Revisit in 6 months.
detail.resumeSummary: 1 year post-grad experience in corporate strategy. Dean's list honors. Strong Excel/SQL skills. Interned at regional logistics firm.

detail.education: MS Supply Chain Management, University of Michigan
detail.opsExposure: Supply chain manager at automotive tier-1 supplier. Owned end-to-end logistics for $40M product line.
detail.leadershipDepth: Managed cross-functional team of 12. Led vendor negotiations saving $2.4M annually. Mentored 3 junior analysts.
detail.leaderNotes: Immediately impressed both interviewers. Deep operational knowledge combined with executive presence. Top candidate.
detail.resumeSummary: 6 years in supply chain and operations. APICS CSCP certified. Track record of cost reduction and process redesign. Published speaker at CSCMP conference.

detail.education: BBA Operations Management, University of Missouri – Columbia
detail.opsExposure: Operations coordinator at regional distribution center. Managed daily throughput for 200+ SKU facility.
detail.leadershipDepth: Shift supervisor for team of 6. Implemented new scheduling system that reduced overtime by 15%.
detail.leaderNotes: Solid operational instincts. Showed good problem-solving in case study. Could benefit from broader strategic exposure.
detail.resumeSummary: 4 years in distribution and warehouse operations. Lean Six Sigma Yellow Belt. Promoted twice in 3 years. Strong references from direct supervisors.

detail.opsExposure: Marketing operations assistant. Exposure to project timelines and vendor coordination but not core ops.
detail.leadershipDepth: No direct leadership experience. Active volunteer coordinator for local nonprofit.
detail.leaderNotes: Personable and enthusiastic but lacks operational depth for current openings. Better fit for a different track.
detail.resumeSummary: 2 years in marketing operations support. Strong communication skills. Organized 3 regional events with 500+ attendees. Looking to transition into core operations.

events.healthSummary: Moderate quality density. Strong early-career representation. Recommend strengthening university targeting for next Midwest event.

events.summary: 11 of 31 RSVPs attended (35% conversion). 7 resumes collected. Heavy operations and management interest. No leadership check-ins were conducted at this event — candidate evaluations pending.
